posting_status: LIVE — HTTP 200 via Apify/LinkedIn, posted ~2 weeks ago, 200+ applicants
comp: Commensurate with skills and experience (no range listed)
sponsorship_note: No explicit 'no sponsorship' statement in JD. LAIKA is a private US studio (Nike-adjacent Portland metro employer); sponsorship status UNKNOWN — keep eligible, flag for Jamie to verify during apply.
jd_summary: Support end users of LAIKA's in-house + 3rd-party technical tools across the animation studio. Work under Technology Engagement & Development Supervisor. Core responsibilities: requirements gathering via story mapping, training delivery for new product rollouts, workflow documentation, test feedback collection, bug reporting communications, web-based resource creation (guides, tutorials, release notes, newsletters), end-user surveys.

key_points.training_delivery: Develop and deliver presentations + training for new product rollouts and feature updates
key_points.documentation: Document workflows; identify training needs; create application guides, tutorials, release notes, change notifications, newsletters
key_points.requirements_gathering: Conduct story mapping sessions with users; create user stories for developers
key_points.stakeholder_surveys: Survey end users on technology use patterns; report findings to developers as user stories
key_points.technical_support: Front-line support to users; verify bugs; test new releases; track and communicate known issues
key_points.tools: MS Office suite + Adobe Creative Suite (required); web dev + video production (plus)
key_points.agile: Experience with agile software development terms and tools (required)
key_points.preferred: Feature animation or VFX production experience; technical writing; web development
key_points.gap_honest: Jamie has zero VFX/animation experience, no agile tooling (no Jira/sprint boards), no pipeline-engineering background, no bug-tracking experience — these are real gaps vs the JD's preferred/required quals. Strengths: training delivery at scale, guide/resource creation, stakeholder needs-gathering, documentation, MS Office + Adobe InDesign/Canva.
